name: All About Reading
description: Multi-sensory, Orton-Gillingham-based phonics and reading program with scripted lessons, letter tiles, and engaging readers
notes: Reading Interactive Kit ($22-44) is a one-time purchase used across all levels. Excellent for struggling readers and dyslexia. Mastery-based progression, not grade-locked. One-year money-back guarantee.

name: Logic of English Foundations
description: Comprehensive phonics, reading, spelling, and handwriting program that teaches the rules behind English spelling with multi-sensory methods
notes: Level A costs more ($228) due to Core Materials one-time purchase; subsequent levels $90-125. Online version available at ~$75/year. Orton-Gillingham based. Essentials series extends through grade 7 for older students. Teaches both cursive and print.

name: The Good and the Beautiful Language Arts
description: Beautifully illustrated, integrated language arts covering reading, grammar, spelling, writing, art, and geography in one program
notes: Many levels available as FREE PDF downloads. Non-denominational Christian with sporadic mentions of God and KJV references. Author is LDS but content is non-denominationally Christian. Placement test recommended as levels run advanced. Some reviewers note weak explicit phonics instruction and reading comprehension gaps.

name: Sonlight Language Arts
description: Literature-rich language arts integrated with grammar, mechanics, dictation, and creative writing using high-quality read-alouds and books
notes: Expensive but includes a mini-library of high-quality books reusable across siblings. Open-and-teach instructor guides with daily lesson plans. Flexible grade levels. One-year money-back guarantee. Heavy on reading — best for book-loving families. Returning customer discounts available.

name: Memoria Press Language Arts
description: Structured classical Christian language arts with emphasis on grammar, Latin, literature guides, and traditional spelling
notes: Complete classical curriculum with strong Latin component. Individual components can be purchased separately to customize. Grade-level packages include literature guides, spelling, grammar, and composition. More affordable than comparable classical programs like Veritas Press. Excellent customer service.

name: IEW Structure and Style
description: Systematic video-based writing curriculum teaching structure and style through incremental, mastery-based instruction
notes: Writing-focused — not a complete language arts program. Needs separate grammar, spelling, and reading programs (Fix It! Grammar available as add-on). Core Structure and Style program is secular-friendly; Christian-themed options also available. Video instruction means minimal parent prep for teaching, but initial learning curve to understand the method. Excellent return policy.

name: BookShark Language Arts
description: Secular, literature-based language arts covering reading, writing, grammar, spelling, vocabulary, and copywork using quality books
notes: Secular spin-off of Sonlight with the same literature-based approach. Four-day-per-week schedule. Uses Explode the Code for early phonics (levels A-C) and Wordly Wise for vocabulary (level C+). All-Subject bundles available at 20% discount. Diverse and modern literature selections. Note: Some users describe BookShark as feeling lightly Christian despite its secular label, possibly due to its Sonlight roots and book selections.

name: Brave Writer
description: Charlotte Mason-inspired writing lifestyle curriculum using copywork, dictation, freewriting, and living literature to develop confident writers
notes: Not a traditional open-and-go curriculum — more of a writing lifestyle approach requiring significant parent involvement. Excellent for reluctant writers. Does NOT cover phonics or handwriting. Online classes ($100-200 per 3-week session) available but add to cost. Age-based bundles: Beginning (5-7), Emerging (8-10), Middle School, High School, College Prep.

name: Learning Language Arts Through Literature
description: Affordable, literature-based all-in-one language arts integrating phonics, grammar, spelling, writing, and reading through classic books
notes: Color-coded books by grade level. Based on Dr. Ruth Beechick's and Charlotte Mason's teaching ideas. Christian content is light and sporadic — some Bible passages, hymns for dictation. Many secular families use it comfortably. Gray Book (8th grade) is most overtly Christian. Composition coverage is considered light — advanced writers may need supplements. Literature books purchased separately.

name: Explode the Code
description: Simple, systematic Orton-Gillingham phonics workbook series that builds reading skills through incremental practice
notes: Budget-friendly phonics supplement, not a complete language arts program. 17 workbooks from primers through Book 8. Students can work mostly independently. Black and white illustrations (some hard to identify). Half-levels available for extra practice. Often used alongside BookShark, Charlotte Mason, or other literature-based programs.

name: All About Spelling
description: Multi-sensory, Orton-Gillingham-based spelling program with scripted lessons, letter tiles, flashcards, and mastery-based progression through seven levels
notes: From the same publisher as All About Reading. Seven mastery-based levels (not grade-locked) — most students start at Level 1 regardless of grade. Requires one-time purchase of Letter Tiles ($26) and Spelling Review Box ($18) used across all levels. Level 1 costs $65; Levels 2-3 are $70; Levels 4-7 are $50 each. Fully scripted teacher manual requires no advance prep. Spelling sessions are 15-20 minutes, 3-4 days/week. Students spell full sentences from Level 1, not just isolated words. Excellent for struggling spellers and students with dyslexia. Parent involvement required (not independent work). Student packet is partially consumable. One-year money-back guarantee. Placement test available on publisher's website.

name: Spelling Power
description: Comprehensive, all-in-one spelling curriculum covering 5,000 most-frequently-used words across all grade levels in a single book, using research-proven strategies and 15-minute daily sessions
notes: One book covers entire family from age 8 through adult — a one-time purchase that lasts a lifetime. Manual only is $65; Basic Pack (manual + 2 student record books + activity task cards) is $103; Deluxe Pack (everything + magnetic letter tiles + word bank box) is $178. Diagnostic placement test determines starting point across 11 levels and 47 word groups. Requires only 15 minutes daily — 5 minutes direct instruction plus 10 minutes student work. Endorsed by International Dyslexia Association. Over 250,000 families served since 1993. Multiple award winner (Practical Homeschooling, Cathy Duffy Top 100). Initial setup can feel overwhelming due to extensive introductory material, but Quick Start guide and DVD help. Student Record Books are consumable ($8-10 each).

name: Saxon Math
description: Decades-proven spiral math curriculum with incremental lessons and daily review for strong retention and high test scores
notes: Gold standard for spiral/procedural math. Constant review builds retention but can feel tedious for quick learners. Dry black-and-white presentation. Lessons are 45-60 min in upper grades. Homeschool kits include textbook, tests, worksheets, and solutions manual. Manipulatives kit ($75-180) required for K-3. Some consider it the reason they love math; others the reason they hate it.

name: Singapore Math (Dimensions)
description: Rigorous mastery-based curriculum using the Concrete-Pictorial-Abstract approach that produces top global math performers
notes: Based on Singapore's world-leading math methodology. Dimensions Math is the newer flagship series (PK-8); Primary Mathematics is the classic (K-6) with multiple editions (US, Standards, 2022). Strong focus on bar modeling, mental math, and conceptual depth. Best for math-confident parents; less-confident parents may want video supplements. Grades 6-8 have a noticeably different presentation than K-5.

name: Math-U-See
description: Multisensory mastery-based program with manipulative blocks and video instruction covering K through Calculus
notes: Uses colored manipulative blocks so students visualize math concepts before working abstractly. Video instruction by Steve Demme teaches each lesson. 13 levels (Primer through Calculus) are mastery-based, not grade-based — students advance when ready. Best for visual/kinesthetic learners and students who struggled with other programs. Some families find the slower mastery pace frustrating; blocks don't help every learner equally.

name: Beast Academy
description: Challenging comic-book-style math curriculum focused on problem-solving and advanced mathematical thinking
notes: Created by the Art of Problem Solving team. Comic-book-style guides with monster characters teach advanced concepts through puzzles and logical reasoning. Online platform ($16/mo or $100/yr) or print books ($27 per guide+practice set). The most challenging elementary math program available. NOT for struggling math students — designed for kids who love math and are easily bored by drills. Levels don't align with traditional grades; start 1-2 levels below current grade. After Beast Academy, students continue with AoPS for middle/high school.

name: RightStart Mathematics
description: Hands-on abacus-based curriculum using games and visualization instead of rote drills, developed by a Montessori educator
notes: Developed by Dr. Joan Cotter, a Montessori educator. Uses the specially designed AL Abacus as its primary tool. 300+ card games replace flash card drills. Scripted lessons are open-and-go but require one-on-one parent instruction (30-45 min daily). Levels A-H (K-8). Initial manipulatives investment ~$218 (Math Set) is a one-time purchase. Excellent for children with learning challenges like dyscalculia. Primarily suited for individual instruction, not group settings.

name: Math Mammoth
description: Affordable mastery-based worktext emphasizing conceptual understanding and self-directed learning with minimal prep
notes: Light Blue Series is the complete curriculum (grades 1-7); Blue Series is topical/supplemental. Written directly to the student for self-teaching. PDF format is printable for siblings at no extra cost — entire K-7 curriculum for ~$150-275 total. Meets/exceeds Common Core Standards. Author Maria Miller excels at simplifying complex math concepts. No built-in hands-on activities or teacher scripts. Best for independent learners with math-comfortable parents. After grade 7, students move to AoPS or traditional Algebra 1 from another publisher.

name: Life of Fred
description: Story-based math series following a child prodigy's hilarious adventures, teaching real-world math through narrative
notes: Students follow Fred Gauss, a 5-year-old math professor at KITTENS University. Hardcover, non-consumable, reusable books (~$200 for 10 elementary books). Created by Dr. Stanley Schmidt. Faith-neutral but includes occasional mentions of God/church — not fully secular. Light on practice problems; supplementing with 'Zillions of Practice Problems' books is recommended. Best as a supplement or for self-motivated readers who hate traditional math. Elementary series has only 38-57 lessons/year, which may not be sufficient as a standalone. Through high school and into college-level topics (linear algebra, statistics).

name: The Good and the Beautiful Math
description: Affordable spiral math with beautiful design and a gentle Christian worldview — much of the digital curriculum is free
notes: Math K-7 digital downloads are free; printed materials are $10-40. General Christian worldview (gratitude, honesty, prayer) without denominational doctrine — produced by members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. Spiral approach revisits concepts regularly. Beautifully designed, colorful materials. Curriculum runs advanced; take the placement test as many students place below their grade. Some lessons are more hands-on than expected. Not suitable for secular families without significant tweaking.

name: Horizons Math
description: Colorful, fast-paced spiral curriculum with hands-on activities and teacher-directed lessons from a Christian publisher
notes: Brightly illustrated consumable workbooks with full-color student pages. Scope and sequence is intentionally advanced — covers concepts earlier than Common Core requires. Teacher handbook outlines every step of each lesson. Heavy use of manipulatives in early grades (dominoes, counters, play money, abacus). Requires one-on-one teacher instruction. Covers K-6 plus a Pre-Algebra course. Complete sets run ~$120/grade. Some parents report concepts being skipped or poorly explained in upper grades.

name: ShillerMath
description: Montessori-based multisensory math with scripted lessons, physical manipulatives, and zero lesson prep required
notes: Three kits cover PK-3 (Kit I, $300-400), 4th-Pre-Algebra (Kit II, $300), and Fractions ($125). Each kit spans 4-5 years so per-year cost is reasonable. Includes 12+ physical manipulatives (balance, number tiles, wooden shapes, decimal cubes, etc.). Completely scripted — just read what's in quotes. 15 min/day for age 4, 30-45 min for age 9+. Lifetime downloads for consumable lessons. Virtual-only options available at $80-125/kit. Limited online reviews. Some parents report retention concerns and inconsistencies in teaching guides.

name: Real Science Odyssey
description: Secular, hands-on science curriculum with labs, experiments, and short-story-format lessons covering life science, earth science, chemistry, physics, and astronomy.
notes: Level 1 (K-6) and Level 2 (6-10). Parents need to prep experiments but lessons are scripted. Teaches evolution and big bang as fact. No built-in videos or multimedia — pencil-and-paper plus labs. Materials are mostly common household items; optional science kits available.

name: Apologia Science
description: Award-winning Christian science curriculum with conversational textbooks, hands-on experiments, and creation-based perspective for K-12.
notes: Most popular Christian homeschool science curriculum for 11+ years running. Young Explorer series (K-6) is multi-age and Charlotte Mason-style. High school courses are college-prep rigorous. Textbooks are conversational and designed for self-teaching. Video lessons available but expensive ($400-600 extra). Creation-based perspective throughout.

name: Building Foundations of Scientific Understanding (BFSU)
description: Inquiry-based, discussion-driven secular science curriculum in 3 volumes that builds deep scientific understanding through critical thinking and hands-on activities.
notes: Considered the 'gold standard' for secular homeschool science. Three volumes covering K-2, 3-5, and 6-8. Extremely budget-friendly. Requires significant parent preparation and some science background building. Discussion-based and inquiry-driven — not scripted. Volumes must be used sequentially. Exceeds NGSS standards. Parents who are scientists often choose this one.

name: Mystery Science
description: Video-based, inquiry-driven secular science curriculum with guided experiments and discussion prompts for elementary students.
notes: Online streaming video lessons with hands-on activities using common materials. NGSS-aligned. Great for visual/kinesthetic learners and parents without science backgrounds. May only work as complete curriculum through grade 2 — better as supplement for grades 3-5. Lessons range from 5-minute mini-lessons to 45-60 minute full lessons.

name: Noeo Science
description: Literature-based science curriculum combining living books, hands-on experiment kits, and Charlotte Mason methods for grades 1-8.
notes: Complete box includes Instructor's Guide, Student Lab Manual, living books, and experiment kits. 2-day or 4-day schedule options. Christian company but curriculum is worldview-neutral by design — used by both secular and Christian families. No quizzes or tests. Three levels: 1-3, 4-6, 7-8. Covers biology, chemistry, and physics. 60-day trial guarantee.

name: Nancy Larson Science
description: Fully scripted, open-and-go elementary science with hands-on kits, structured lessons, and spiral review — by the author of Saxon Math K-3.
notes: Most scripted science curriculum available — parent reads lesson script aloud while student follows in booklet. Includes complete kit with manipulatives, word wall cards, charts, assessments. Designed 2-3 days/week. Spiral approach revisits topics with increasing depth. Premium price but kids reportedly love it and ask to do science. Best for parents who dread teaching science.

name: Berean Builders
description: Christian creation-based science by Dr. Jay Wile covering K-12 with experiments, multi-level review questions, and science history integration.
notes: By the original Apologia author Dr. Jay Wile. Elementary books cover science through history (5 books, ~3 years each). Three levels of review questions let you use same book across grade ranges. Middle/high school courses are rigorous (Chemistry requires Algebra I). Budget-friendly. Author personally answers student questions via email. Best for grades 3-6+ at elementary level despite K label.

name: Sonlight Science
description: Literature-based Christian science curriculum with living books, Discover & Do experiment kits, and fully planned daily schedules.
notes: Christian company but Science E and some other levels use secular books — parents can share their own viewpoint. Each level spans 3-year age range for multi-age teaching. Discover & Do kits include experiment supplies. 4-day or 5-day schedule options. Presents both creation and evolution perspectives. Reading-heavy — requires parent read-aloud time.

name: Oak Meadow Science
description: Waldorf-inspired secular science woven into a holistic K-12 curriculum emphasizing nature connection, artistic integration, and experiential learning.
notes: Founded 1975 by a trained Waldorf teacher. Science separate from grade 5+; integrated K-4. Nature-focused assignments in early grades. Optional accredited distance learning school with teacher support. 36-week lesson plans with assignment checklists. Expensive for full packages. May feel slow for gifted/advanced learners. Best for creative and kinesthetic learners who thrive with artistic integration.

name: BookShark Science
description: Secular, literature-based science curriculum aligned to NGSS with living books, hands-on activities, and 4-day weekly schedule.
notes: Secular spin-off of Sonlight. NGSS-aligned since 2021 revision. Includes science kit, instructor's guide, and assorted topical books. DVD experiment demos for levels A-E. 36-week, 4-day schedule. Religiously neutral — encourages parent-led worldview discussions. Reusable across siblings (only consumables need replacing). Can be paired with BookShark History for a full secular package. Note: Some users describe BookShark as feeling lightly Christian despite its secular label, possibly due to its Sonlight roots.

name: Story of the World
description: Engaging narrative world history in 4 chronological volumes with activity books, maps, coloring pages, and audiobook options.
notes: The most popular elementary homeschool history curriculum. Four volumes: Ancient Times, Middle Ages, Early Modern Times, Modern Age. Faith-friendly (covers Biblical events as history) but not faith-based — treats all religions evenhandedly. Activity books are optional and extensive — choose what fits your schedule. Available in print, digital, and audiobook. Best for grades 1-4 as read-aloud; 5-8 can use independently with activity book. Volume 4 has fewer hands-on activities.

name: The Mystery of History
description: Chronological world history from a biblical worldview in 4 volumes with multi-age activities, timeline, and mapwork for K-12.
notes: Classical, chronological, Christian approach. Activities divided into younger/middle/older age ranges for multi-age family use. 28-week curriculum, 2-3 lessons per week. Companion Guide includes mapwork, timelines, assessments, and book lists. Vol 1: Creation to Resurrection, Vol 2: Early Church to Middle Ages, Vol 3: Renaissance to Growth of Nations, Vol 4: Wars of Independence to Modern Times. High schoolers need supplemental reading and a separate US History course. Can feel dry/fact-intensive for very young students (K-3).

name: Beautiful Feet Books
description: Charlotte Mason-inspired, literature-based history using living books with teacher guides, narration, notebooking, and timeline activities.
notes: Founded 1984. Literature packs include teacher guide + 10-20 living books per course. Lessons organized by topic (not 5-day weeks) for flexibility. Heavy on narration (oral and written), notebooking, and map/timeline work. Christian company but curriculum could be adapted for secular use. Can cover history + geography + writing + art in early grades. No worksheets or tests. High school Modern History counts as 2 credits (History + Language Arts). Can use library books to reduce cost.

name: Notgrass History
description: Visually rich, photo-heavy Christian history curriculum integrating Bible study, language arts, and hands-on projects for grades 1-12.
notes: Three tiers: Elementary (grades 1-4), Middle School (5-8), High School (9-12). Includes Bible study and language arts assignments within history lessons. Photo-heavy and vibrant — great for visual learners. No separate teacher's guide needed — all instructions included. Middle/high school students can work independently. Titles: Our Star-Spangled Story, Our 50 States, From Adam to Us, America the Beautiful, Exploring America, Exploring World History, etc. Covers US history, world history, geography, government, and economics.

name: Tapestry of Grace
description: Comprehensive classical Christian unit-study curriculum using history as the organizing theme for literature, writing, fine arts, geography, and Bible across K-12.
notes: Award-winning eclectic curriculum blending Classical, Charlotte Mason, unit studies, and lapbooking. Four classical learning levels: lower grammar (K-3), upper grammar (3-6), dialectic (6-9), rhetoric (9-12). Four-year cycle through world history — all ages study same period simultaneously. Covers history, writing, literature, fine arts, geography, church history, Bible. Does NOT include math, science, phonics, foreign language. Heavy parent prep (1-2 hours/week pre-reading). Weekly plans ~50 pages. Best value for multi-age families planning to use through high school. Free 3-week sample available.

name: History Quest
description: Secular, story-format world history read-aloud with optional study guides covering ancient through modern times.
notes: From the makers of Real Science Odyssey. Chapter-book format designed as family read-aloud (grades 1-4) or independent read (5+). Volumes: Early Times, Middle Times, Modern Times, plus US History & Civics. Study guides sold separately with activities, maps, and timeline work. History Odyssey bridge series for grades 6-12. Neutral on religion — presents events based on historical facts. 3 weeks of free 'try before you buy.' Very budget-friendly. Same publisher as RSO — pairs well for secular families.

name: BookShark History
description: Secular, literature-based history curriculum using diverse living books, historical fiction, and biographies with fully planned 4-day schedules.
notes: Secular spin-off of Sonlight. Levels A-J (PreK through 10th grade). History & Literature integrated — students read ~50 books/year. Level F (Eastern history) is especially beloved. Religiously neutral — covers faith's historical role from multiple perspectives. 36-week, 4-day schedule with detailed instructor's guide. Includes read-alouds, independent readers, timeline and map resources. Reusable across siblings. Can pair with BookShark Science for full secular package. Virtual option available for online tracking. Note: Some users describe BookShark as feeling lightly Christian despite its secular label, possibly due to its Sonlight roots and book selections.

name: Sonlight History
description: Christian literature-based history using living books, historical fiction, and read-alouds with fully planned daily schedules and timeline resources.
notes: Originally designed for missionary families overseas. History/Bible/Literature integrated into one package. Christian perspective but explores world religions and cultures. Each level spans 3-year age range for multi-age teaching. 4-day or 5-day schedule options. Extensive reading load — requires dedicated parent read-aloud time. Expensive but books are reusable. Save 15-20% with All-Subjects Package. Payment plans available.

name: Simply Charlotte Mason History
description: Charlotte Mason-method history using a 6-year cycle, living books, narration, and nature study for the whole family grades 1-12.
notes: Pure Charlotte Mason approach — living books, narration, short lessons, nature study. 6-year history cycle covering ancient through modern. One teacher guide works for whole family grades 1-12 with book suggestions per form/level. Christian perspective but gentle integration. Budget-friendly since many books can be sourced from libraries. Pairs well with SCM's other subjects (geography, nature study, Bible). Best for families specifically seeking Charlotte Mason methodology.

name: Moving Beyond the Page Social Studies
description: Secular, literature-based social studies integrating language arts through thematic units with hands-on projects and critical thinking activities.
notes: Standards-based secular curriculum that goes beyond public school basics. Integrates social studies with language arts through literature. Thematic units with hands-on projects and critical thinking emphasis. Available for ages 7-14 in online or print format. Strong fit for project-based and gifted learners. Less well-known but highly regarded by secular homeschool community.

name: Sonlight
description: Literature-rich Christian curriculum with comprehensive read-aloud-based lesson plans covering all subjects in one package
notes: All-Subjects Packages include history, Bible, geography, read-alouds, readers, language arts, math (Math-U-See default, swappable), and science. 4- or 5-day schedule options. Very reading-intensive — requires significant daily parent read-aloud time. Books are reusable for younger siblings. Love to Learn guarantee allows 18-week trial with full refund.

name: BookShark
description: Secular literature-based curriculum (sister company to Sonlight) with comprehensive all-subject packages and a 4-day schedule
notes: Secular alternative to Sonlight. All-Subject Packages cover language arts, science, math, history, and geography. 4-day/36-week schedule. Students read ~50 books per year. Does not cover high school fully (ends around age 16/level J). Heavy reading load requires parent involvement for read-alouds. Books reusable for siblings. Note: Some users describe BookShark as feeling lightly Christian despite its secular label, possibly due to its Sonlight roots and book selections — parents with strong secular preferences should review book lists carefully.

name: Timberdoodle
description: Hands-on, thinking-skills-focused curriculum kits curated from hundreds of publishers, available in Christian and non-religious editions
notes: Available in three tiers: Basic ($395-580, core subjects only), Complete ($800-990, all subjects), and Elite (premium extras). Both Classic (Christian) and Non-Religious editions at same price. Strong emphasis on STEM, logic puzzles, and thinking skills. Kit Customizer tool lets you swap items before purchasing. Does not include foreign language or literature book sets. Emphasizes independent learning as early as possible.

name: The Good and the Beautiful
description: Affordable, beautifully designed Christian curriculum covering all subjects with integrated family-style history and science units
notes: Exceptionally affordable — Language Arts levels 1-5 available as FREE PDF downloads. Courses typically $10-125 each. History and science are family-style (teach multiple ages together). Language Arts integrates multiple subjects (reading, spelling, grammar, writing, art, geography). Faith content is general Christian worldview, not denomination-specific. Can feel advanced for listed grade level — take placement test. Some reviewers note limited depth and US-centric content.

name: My Father's World
description: Christian curriculum blending Charlotte Mason, classical education, and unit studies with a global missions perspective
notes: Teaches multiple children together via Family Learning Cycle (grades 2-8 learn same history/science, different depth). History is the backbone with other subjects integrated around it. Math and language arts are separate purchases from other publishers (e.g., Spelling Power, Saxon). Bible and Christian values woven into every subject with a global missions emphasis. 4-day schedule. Lots of reading required. Cathy Duffy Top Pick.

name: Oak Meadow
description: Secular Waldorf-inspired curriculum emphasizing creativity, nature, and experiential learning with optional accredited enrollment
notes: Founded 1975 by Waldorf-trained teacher. Covers all subjects including art and music. K-3 coursebooks include all subjects; grades 5-8 have separate coursebooks per subject; high school uses Saxon Math and texts from secular publishers. Optional accredited distance learning school ($1500-2500/year) with teacher evaluations and transcripts. Gentle pacing in early years with increasingly rigorous academics later. May frustrate gifted/fast learners. Heavy on written responses and long-form assignments.

name: Gather 'Round Homeschool
description: Charlotte Mason-inspired Christian unit study curriculum designed for teaching all ages together with minimal prep
notes: Covers all subjects EXCEPT math (separate math program now available). One purchase works for ALL children in the family. Units are 20 lessons (~4-5 weeks each), organized in a 4-year cycle. Covers grammar, spelling, writing, art, reading, comprehension, geography, science, social studies, history, and Bible. Digital or print options. Not accredited. Cost can add up for single-child families but is very economical for large families. High school science coverage may need supplementation.

name: Calvert Homeschool
description: Secular, structured curriculum with 125+ years of history, offering print-based K-2 and online courses for grades 3-12
notes: Founded 1897. Print-based for K-2, online platform for grades 3-12 with 45+ courses, videos, and interactive exercises. 160 lessons + 20 review lessons per course with built-in assessments. Optional Calvert Academy accreditation ($1500-3200/year) with NCAA approval and recognized diplomas. Mastery-based approach with step-by-step lesson plans. Only 5-15 minutes daily parent prep. Subjects are thoughtfully cross-referenced (e.g., Civil War history paired with Whitman poetry in LA).

name: Master Books
description: Affordable, open-and-go Christian curriculum with Charlotte Mason-inspired short lessons and a strong biblical worldview
notes: Very affordable for a complete curriculum. Four-subject bundles available (math, language arts, history, science). 180 daily lessons per subject, ~1 page per day. Biblical worldview deeply integrated into every subject — cannot be separated from content. Does not align with Common Core. Some parents find lessons too easy/gentle. Optional Master Books Academy online videos available for self-paced instruction. Good for special needs and reluctant learners due to gentle approach.

name: Moving Beyond the Page
description: Secular, literature-based integrated curriculum designed for gifted and creative learners with interdisciplinary unit studies
notes: Organized by age range rather than grade (ages 4-5 through 12-14). 3-week interdisciplinary units combining language arts, science, and social studies around central themes. Math curriculum available for some levels but not all — may need to supplement. Hands-on and project-based with dioramas, cooking, experiments, essays. Online version allows multiple siblings at no extra cost. Used by 100+ accredited distance learning schools. Particularly strong for gifted learners. 4-day schedule. Ages 9+ become increasingly independent.

name: Daily Wonder
description: Waldorf-rooted homeschool curriculum created by three experienced Waldorf teachers, integrating all academic subjects through story, hands-on activities, movement, music, and visual/practical arts in daily morning lesson plans
notes: Created by Canadian Waldorf teachers who built and ran a Waldorf school. Digital PDF curriculum with 10 units per year (15 lesson days + 5 flex days each). Each day includes opening verse, movement, math, recall/review, new material, bookwork, story, and closing verse. Daily time commitment is 1-2 hours. Includes access to Wonder Hub with resource videos and supportive community. Individual units available at $44 each to trial. Also offers supplementary ukulele, recorder, pentatonic flute, and handwork courses. Aligned to BC Ministry of Education learning standards (translatable to other provinces/states). Block-teaching model typical of Waldorf education.

name: Earthschooling
description: Award-winning, secular Waldorf curriculum integrating nature studies, storytelling, painting, handwork, Eurythmy, and academics through hands-on activities and natural rhythms, with over 100 hours of teacher support tutorials
notes: The first online Waldorf curriculum provider, operating since 1994. Three purchase tiers: Core Curriculum (PDFs/images/MP3s), Curriculum Package (adds teacher video tutorials for crafts, watercolor, chalk drawing), and Living Lessons (adds video of Waldorf teacher teaching your student). Kindergarten package is $200; upper grades run $250-325. Lifetime Family Package (PreK-G8) is $2,225 without Living Lessons or $4,500 with them. High school (G9-12) is a separate $750 add-on converted to Waldorf School Online. Includes Eurythmy (unique among homeschool curricula), French lessons, music, international/multi-cultural content. Provides alternate lesson blocks for cultural sensitivity (e.g., secular alternatives to Saints block). 30% discount for AU/CA/NZ families. Scholarships available (up to 50% off). Lifetime access includes all future updates. Weekly Q&A sessions with Waldorf teacher included. Accepted by universities for transcripts.

name: Torchlight Curriculum
description: Literature-based, secular humanist curriculum emphasizing diverse authors, Socratic discussion, critical thinking, and hands-on projects across language arts, social studies, science, art, and music
notes: Covers Pre-K through Level 5 (ages 4-14) plus individual Constellation Studies for older students. Does NOT include math or explicit reading/phonics instruction (after Pre-K) — families must supplement. PDF-only curriculum; books purchased separately or borrowed from library. Level Pre-K is $32; Levels K-2 are $40-42; Level 3 is $47; Level 4 is $47; Level 5 is $52 ($76 with art and finance bundle). 36 weeks of daily lessons per level. Strong emphasis on diverse literature and multicultural perspectives. Socratic-inspired discussion and inquiry-based learning throughout. Includes art, music, philosophy, game logic, and creative writing (NaNoWriMo novel project in Levels 4-5). Cathy Duffy Top 103 Pick. Explicitly secular humanist — addresses worldview topics from humanist perspective, not religiously neutral. Heavy reading load; significant parent involvement required for discussions and activities. Very affordable curriculum guide, but book costs can add $100-200 per level if not using library.

name: Curiosity Chronicles
description: Secular, globally inclusive world history curriculum spanning Ancient through Modern history, written in an engaging dialogue-driven format between two child characters. Created by a homeschooling mother with a master's degree in Comparative Studies, it deliberately avoids a Eurocentric perspective and is designed with neurodivergent learners in mind.
notes: Four volumes: Ancient (Gr 1–3), Medieval (Gr 2–5), Early Modern (Gr 3–6), Modern (Gr 4–8). Each volume available à la carte (textbook, audio, timeline kit ~$39) or as complete print package (~$89) including hardcover text, instructor guide, student workbook, audiobook, and wall timeline. Also available as PDF. Minecraft building challenges are a signature feature (requires separate activity guide). Explicitly designed for neurodivergent learners — ADHD, dyslexia, and sensory sensitivities are accommodated in the core curriculum, not as afterthoughts. Dialogue-based narrative style may feel juvenile for students 10+ but is praised for accessibility. Teaches religion as historical context only, with no faith worldview. Widely considered the most genuinely non-Eurocentric homeschool history option available. Use code HOMEGRAD for 5% off.

name: Stories in History
description: Secular, literature-rich, hands-on social studies curriculum designed for homeschool families, emphasizing diverse perspectives and historical thinking. Each full-year course uses picture books and short chapter books as primary sources, paired with projects, maps, timelines, and activities — with very little prep required of parents.
notes: Five full-year courses: My World My Story (Gr 1–2), Early American History (Gr 3–5), California History (Gr 4, CA standards-aligned), Modern American History (Gr 5–8, covers 1870–1968), and Ancient Civilizations (Gr 6, coming Spring 2026). Each course is 8 units, full color, available in digital ($69) or print ($99). Designed for family-style learning — multiple grade levels can participate together. Individual worksheets and activities also sold separately ($1.50–5). Charter funds and ESA funds accepted. Ancient Civilizations course has an optional supplementary Bible Reference Blog, but the main curriculum is secular. Created by a homeschool parent; newer and smaller publisher but with strong parent reviews. Particularly strong for US history and California history; global coverage is limited compared to world history-focused curricula.

name: Waldorf Essentials
description: Complete Waldorf-inspired homeschool curriculum featuring main lesson blocks, form drawing, watercolor painting, handwork, and circle time. Includes Thinking-Feeling-Willing teacher training, weekly coaching calls, and community support. Written by homeschoolers for homeschoolers with 14+ years of experience.
notes: The most comprehensive Waldorf-specific homeschool curriculum available. Explores world religions culturally (not devotionally) as part of the Waldorf tradition. Includes TFW (Thinking-Feeling-Willing) teacher training with 17 modules. Hard copy and online formats. Coaching calls included with curriculum purchase.

name: Lavender's Blue Homeschool
description: Secular Waldorf-inspired curriculum with detailed daily lesson plans featuring main lesson blocks, form drawing, watercolor painting, beeswax modeling, handwork, and recorder. Created by a former kindergarten teacher with a background in developmental neuropsychology. Includes MP3 audio tracks, community membership, and step-by-step guidance for bringing Waldorf to life at home.
notes: Highly detailed secular Waldorf curriculum. Kindergarten is 464 pages + 75 MP3s; First Grade is 541 pages + 63 MP3s; Second Grade is 735 pages + 59 MP3s. Created by Kelly, a former mixed-age kindergarten teacher with developmental neuropsychology background. Emphasizes rhythm, imagination, and play. Digital format (view online, download, or print). Community membership included. Fourth grade in development.

name: Christopherus Homeschool Resources
description: One of the original Waldorf homeschool curricula with deep Steiner/Anthroposophical grounding. Complete grade-by-grade curriculum covering all subjects through main lesson blocks, form drawing, watercolor, handwork, and seasonal rhythms.
notes: One of the oldest Waldorf homeschool curricula. Donna Simmons is a trained Waldorf teacher. Includes consulting component. Deep Steiner philosophy grounding.

name: Live Education
description: Complete Waldorf curriculum packages sold as physical sets per grade. WASC-accredited. Covers all core subjects through the Waldorf developmental approach with main lesson blocks, arts integration, and seasonal rhythms.
notes: WASC-accredited Waldorf curriculum. Complete physical curriculum sets per grade.

name: Logos Press Homeschool Bundles
description: Pioneer of classical Christian education since 1981. Complete curriculum bundles for grades 1-6 with daily lesson plans covering math, science, English, grammar, literature, history, Bible, and Latin starting in 3rd grade.
notes: Associated with Douglas Wilson and Logos School, Moscow, Idaho. Pioneer of classical Christian ed revival. Daily lesson plans included. Latin starts grade 3.

name: The Gentle + Classical Press
description: The gentlest classical option for young children, blending Charlotte Mason's literature-rich methods with classical trivium structure. Covers language arts, history, science, nature study, poetry, art, and music.
notes: Multiple award winner. Blends CM methods with classical structure. Math and reading programs must be sourced separately for K-6. Preschool curriculum available for ages 2-4.

name: Build Your Library
description: The most established secular Charlotte Mason curriculum. 36-week PDF lesson plans covering history, literature, poetry, science, art, nature study, and composer/artist study. Chronological history progression across all levels.
notes: Created by Emily Cook, 21-year homeschool veteran and co-founder of SEA Homeschoolers. Secular. Math and grammar sourced separately. Books from library. Author of 'A Literary Education'.

name: Wildwood Curriculum
description: Modern secular Charlotte Mason curriculum with deliberately diverse, modern book selections. Covers history, geography, literature, science, nature study, art, music, poetry, and handicrafts.
notes: Free. Deliberately chooses books that are secular, modern, and respectful of minorities, indigenous peoples, and women. US, Canadian, and India versions. Companion podcast: Stonechats.

name: Mater Amabilis
description: The premier Catholic Charlotte Mason curriculum, free and online since 2004. Based directly on Mason's Parents' Union School programmes. Covers all CM subjects including history, geography, science, literature, religion, poetry, artist/composer study.
notes: Free Catholic CM curriculum. Named after the Catholic parish in Ambleside, England where Charlotte Mason lived. Literature-rich with extensive read-aloud lists. Thousands of families use it.

name: Living Books Press
description: Charlotte Mason curriculum with 20+ years of development. Week-by-week teaching guides covering Bible, language arts, history, geography, science, nature study, art, and music. 100% of proceeds fund schools educating 750 children in Africa.
notes: 100% of proceeds support schools in Africa. Created by Sheila Carroll with master's degrees in Educational Leadership and Children's Literature. Math recommended separately (Singapore Math or Math-U-See).

name: A Mind in the Light
description: Secular Charlotte Mason curriculum with some classical methods. Organized by historical time period with teaching guides covering science, natural history, literature, tales, poetry, art, music, Plutarch, and citizenship.
notes: Secular CM with some classical methods. Includes French, German, and Latin resources. Still growing — creator building out all historical periods.

name: Wayfarers
description: A 'Classical Mason' blend — four-year historical cycle covering all subjects for the whole family in one guide. Daily lesson plans for three age groups on 2-page spreads. Secular but inclusive of optional religious readings.
notes: Four volumes: Ancient, Medieval, Revolution, Modern. One guide covers the whole family. Secular but includes optional readings from multiple religious traditions. Free 3-week samples available.

name: KONOS Character Curriculum
description: The first curriculum written specifically for homeschool (1983). Hands-on, multi-age unit studies centered on character traits integrating science, social studies, art, music, literature, and health. Each unit revolves around a character trait studied for 1-2 months.
notes: First homeschool curriculum (1983). Three volumes with 30 interchangeable units. Character-trait-based. Math/spelling/grammar purchased separately. Multi-age design for teaching all children together.

name: Five in a Row
publisher: Five in a Row Publishing (Jane Claire Lambert)
description: Award-winning literature-based curriculum where each week centers on one outstanding children's book, extracting lessons in five subject areas across five days. Gentle, affordable, and beloved by homeschool families for decades.
notes: Before FIAR (ages 2-4), Volumes 1-3 (ages 5-9), Volumes 4-8 (ages 9-12+). Optional Bible supplement sold separately. Math/phonics supplements needed for early grades. Books sourced from library.

name: Keys of the Universe
description: The most comprehensive Montessori elementary curriculum for homeschoolers. Complete downloadable albums for all subjects — mathematics, geometry, geography, history, biology, language/literature, art, and music — with video instruction and community support.
notes: Complete Montessori Cosmic Education albums. One-time purchase with permanent access and updates. No prior Montessori training needed. Active support community included.

name: Multisori
description: Authentic Montessori curriculum designed for parents without certification. Covers language arts, mathematics, practical life, science, geography/culture, art, and sensorial activities. All manipulatives are printable — no expensive Montessori materials needed.
notes: One-time purchase. All manipulatives printable. Claims children exit performing 2 years above grade level. Free group coaching included. Scholarship program available.
